name: read_file
description: Read the contents of a file from the agent's workspace. The output is bounded by `max_bytes` (default 32 KiB) and an optional `max_lines` cap so that a single read cannot blow the local-model context window (SECURITY.md threat #5, PRD §4, PHILOSOPHY.md §5).

path.description: Absolute or harness-relative path to the file.

max_bytes.description: Maximum number of UTF-8 bytes to include in the returned `content`. If the file (or the slice from `offset`) is larger than this, the read is truncated and `truncated` is set to true. Defaults to 32768 (32 KiB), which keeps a single read well under the 4K-32K context window of typical local 7B-70B models. Honored before `max_lines` is applied; the effective cap is min(max_bytes, max_lines * average_line_length).

max_lines.description: Optional cap on the number of lines returned. If the file has more lines than this (counted from `offset`), the read is truncated and `truncated` is set to true. Useful when byte count is a poor proxy (e.g. minified JSON, generated code with very long lines).

offset.description: Byte offset (must lie on a UTF-8 character boundary) at which to begin reading. Use together with `max_bytes` to page through a large file across multiple read_file calls.

content.description: File contents, capped at min(max_bytes, bytes_remaining_from_offset). Empty if `offset >= bytes_total`. Length in bytes is always `<= max_bytes` when `truncated` is true.

sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the bytes actually returned in `content` (not the full file). Lets the caller detect changes between calls and confirm page alignment.

truncated.description: true iff the returned `content` does not include the full file (or the remaining file from `offset`). Set by the contract when (a) the byte slice from `offset` exceeds `max_bytes`, OR (b) the line count from `offset` exceeds `max_lines`. The agent MUST branch on this value: re-read with a larger `max_bytes`, re-read from a different `offset`, or stop. Never ignore a true value.

bytes_returned.description: Number of UTF-8 bytes in `content`. Always `<= max_bytes` when `truncated` is true.

bytes_total.description: Total size of the file on disk in bytes, independent of `offset` and `max_bytes`. Useful for sizing follow-up reads.

truncation_policy.rationale: 32 KiB of UTF-8 text is roughly 8K-16K tokens depending on the tokenizer. This keeps one read_file call well below the 4K-32K context window of typical local 7B-70B models (PRD §4) and bounds a single bad read so it cannot exhaust context (SECURITY.md threat #5). Phase 3 will add historical-log pruning; this per-call safety bound is the Phase 1 minimum.
truncation_policy.behavior_when_truncated: Cut at the largest byte position `<= max_bytes` that ends on a '\n' boundary; emit sha256 over the returned slice; set truncated=true; populate bytes_returned and bytes_total. When max_lines is set and is the binding constraint, cut after that many lines regardless of byte position.
